envelope.sourceCitation: FAA Guide for Aviation Medical Examiners, Item 36 — Hypertension

thresholdDescription: The AME Guide directs further evaluation of hypertension when blood pressure is elevated; a commonly cited evaluation trigger is around 155/95 mmHg. The figure shown is descriptive context, not a pass/fail line — your reading is shown next to the cited figure so you can see where you stand and discuss it with your AME.

envelope.sourceCitation: FAA Guide for Aviation Medical Examiners, Item 36 — Hypertension

thresholdDescription: For Class 3 airmen the AME Guide similarly directs evaluation of elevated blood pressure (a commonly cited trigger around 155/95 mmHg). Descriptive context only, not a pass/fail line.

envelope.sourceCitation: FAA Guide for Aviation Medical Examiners, Item 34 / Item 47 — OSA screening

thresholdDescription: The FAA does not set a weight or BMI pass/fail limit for certification. BMI is only one input an AME may consider when deciding whether to screen for obstructive sleep apnea. Your weight is shown as an informational trend for your own awareness; there is no FAA threshold to meet.

envelope.sourceCitation: FAA Guide for Aviation Medical Examiners — Cardiovascular evaluation

thresholdDescription: The AME Guide does not set a numeric pass/fail resting heart rate. Unusually high or low rates, or an abnormal rhythm, are evaluated in clinical context (e.g., via ECG and history). Your resting heart rate is shown as informational trend data; there is no cited threshold number.

envelope.sourceCitation: FAA Guide for Aviation Medical Examiners (no specific HRV criterion)

thresholdDescription: Heart rate variability is not an FAA certification metric and the AME Guide cites no threshold for it. It is shown here purely as informational trend data for your own awareness; it carries no FAA pass/fail value.

envelope.sourceCitation: FAA Guide for Aviation Medical Examiners, Item 47 — Sleep Disturbances

thresholdDescription: The FAA does not set a numeric sleep-duration threshold. Sleep is addressed through obstructive sleep apnea screening and the sleep-disturbances disposition (Item 47), not a nightly-hours pass/fail. Your sleep total is informational.

envelope.sourceCitation: FAA Guide for Aviation Medical Examiners — Pulmonary evaluation

thresholdDescription: The AME Guide does not set a consumer-device SpO2 pass/fail value for certification. Oxygen saturation is relevant clinically (e.g., in pulmonary or sleep evaluation) but there is no cited wearable-SpO2 threshold. Shown as informational trend data.

envelope.sourceCitation: FAA Guide for Aviation Medical Examiners — ECG requirements (Item 36)

thresholdDescription: An ECG is required for certain classes and ages (for example, first-class airmen at specified ages), and abnormal findings are evaluated case by case. There is no single numeric pass/fail value — the relevant output is the clinical interpretation/classification, not a number.

envelope.sourceCitation: FAA Guide for Aviation Medical Examiners (no specific VO2 max criterion)

thresholdDescription: VO2 max / cardio fitness is not an FAA certification metric and the AME Guide cites no threshold for it. Shown here as informational trend data only; it has no FAA pass/fail value.
